# zkCoins

> zkCoins is a self-custodial wallet for **Shielded CSV**, a privacy protocol for Bitcoin. Send and receive **real BTC** while the chain reveals only a **64-byte nullifier** — amounts, sender, receiver and transaction history stay private, verified client-side with zero-knowledge proofs. No token, no sidechain, no soft fork.

## Key facts
- zkCoins moves native Bitcoin (BTC). It is **not** a token, altcoin, sidechain, or bridge to another chain.
- Shielded CSV works on Bitcoin **as it exists today** — no soft fork, no hard fork, no consensus change.
- Each transfer writes only a **64-byte nullifier** to the chain (~8.75× smaller than a normal transaction), lifting effective throughput from ~11 to ~100 transactions per second.
- **Self-custodial**: keys are generated and stored locally in the browser and never leave the device. Peer-to-peer, no coordinator or custodian.
- Based on the paper *Shielded CSV: Private and Efficient Client-Side Validation* by Jonas Nick, Liam Eagen and Robin Linus (IACR ePrint 2025/068).

## Core pages
- [zkCoins home](https://zkcoins.com/): overview, how it works, roadmap, FAQ
- [Wallet](https://zkcoins.app): the self-custodial zkCoins wallet (web / PWA)
- [Documentation](https://docs.zkcoins.app): architecture, protocol, privacy model, wallet guide
- [Brand kit](https://github.com/zk-coins/landing-page/tree/main/brand): logo, color, typography, voice, design tokens

## Protocol
- [Shielded CSV whitepaper — ePrint 2025/068](https://eprint.iacr.org/2025/068)
- [ZeroSync prototype](https://github.com/ZeroSync/ZKCoins)
- [Source code](https://github.com/zk-coins)
